name: Thread Pool
def: A managed set of reusable worker threads that execute submitted tasks from a queue.
problem: Avoid the cost of creating/destroying threads per task and bound concurrency.
mechanism: Workers pull tasks from a shared (or per-worker, work-stealing) queue; the pool size bounds parallelism and reuses threads.
limits: Blocking tasks can exhaust the pool
failures: Pool exhaustion from blocking I/O on pool threads
whenNot: Tiny one-off tasks; pure async I/O (event loop)
myths: "More threads = faster" — beyond cores, contention and switching dominate
